text: Safely and effectively operate a mosquito control spray truck as part of an integrated mosquito
The following duties are typical for this position. Incumbents may not perform all of the listed duties and/or may be
required to perform additional or different duties from those set forth below to address business needs and changing
 Thoroughly spray assigned zones as instructed by the contract supervisor
 Properly use GPS monitor equipment as instructed, and report any malfunctions immediately
 Accurately record all required data from each spray mission on the appropriate log sheet
 Be thoroughly familiar with all applicable chemical labels and Material Safety Data Sheets
(MSDS), make sure copies of both documents, for all chemicals present, are in the truck at all
times, and handle/apply all chemicals according to label specifications
 Know and follow proper safety and spill procedures at all times, and use all required Personal
 Report all incidents or accidents to contract supervisor immediately
 Communicate respectfully, honestly, and knowledgeably to residents if stopped and
 Keep truck clean and free of trash or debris at all times
 Never allow a passenger, other than another Calhoun county employee in truck at any time
 Fully support, enforce, promote and implement the County's safety policies and programs
 React to change promptly and effectively, and handle other essential tasks as assigned
 High school diploma or GED and be at least 18 years or older
 Must have and maintain a clean driving record.
 Authorized to work in the United States
 Must pass a pre-employment drug screen
 Communicate effectively in English in both written and verbal forms
Ability to follow directions and navigate safely on roadways.
Ability to complete assignment within the project time period.
Ability to utilize a laptop, and/or comparable device.
Ability to communicate and deal effectively with the public.
Ability to work under stressful conditions.
 Ability to interact effectively with others.
Ability to traverse undeveloped, uneven, wet, broken terrain, wooded, marshy areas.
Ability to see, talk, hear, reach, walk, bend, squat, kneel, grasp. Peripheral vision.
Visual acuity (peripheral vision and depth perception) necessary to operate a motorized vehicle.
Ability to read and comprehend manuals; instructions, procedures, memos, letters.
Ability to perform shop math. Ability to speak clearly and concisely to public and laymen.
 Ability to write: summaries, instructions, procedures and required documentation.
 Ability to tolerate inclement weather including rain, humidity, heat, and cold.
 Potential exposure to chemicals and confined spaces and poor ventilation
The work requires strenuous physical exertion and the employee may be required to
drive, stand, walk, bend, climb, kneel and crouch for extended periods of time, as well WORK
ENVIRONMENT as see, talk, hear and use hands and arms to grasp, handle, reach and feel. The
employee must be capable of lifting and using heavy items
HOURS / SHIFTS Seasonal work with a Maximum of 29 hours per week
BENEFITS No Health Benefits included. We are an FRS employer; therefore, retirement included
